mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice 'fast paths' for setting up a secure and minimal viable cluster. While it is user-friendly, it requires familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and may not cover all advanced configurations.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

```

> Add Google Cloud public key for package verification.
> Add Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list to include Kubernetes packages.
> Install specific versions of kubeadm, kubelet, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This step initializes the control plane node of the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=10.244.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with Flannel pod network.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory in the home folder.
> Copy the admin config to the kube directory.
> Change ownership of the config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coreos/flannel/master/Documentation/k8s-manifests/kube-flannel.yml

```

> Install Flannel as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset Kubeadm Configuration
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated configurations. Data loss may occur, and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

rm $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove kubeconfig directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current cluster state, drain the node, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node status before upgrade.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Apply the upgrade to the cluster.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all nodes are ready after upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet service may not be started or is failing.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources or incompatible OS configuration.

**Fix:**
Ensure minimum 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node, and check OS compatibility.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network issues or incorrect image name.

**Fix:**
Check network connectivity and verify the image name in the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Pod network add-on may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the pod network add-on is installed and running correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features like 'kubelet config file', improved upgrade process.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new kubelet configuration options.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
